title: Rate My Professors - Professor Search
text: The Rate My Professors search page for Livingstone College lists 21 professors connected to the school. The page shows professor names, departments, quality ratings, number of ratings, percentage of students who would take the professor again, and level of difficulty. Examples shown on the page include James Fakunle in Business with a quality score of 5.0 from 2 ratings, Karam Batra in Mathematics with a quality score of 5.0 from 2 ratings, Cyprian Alozie in Economics with a quality score of 1.0 from 1 rating, Curtis Dean in Business with a quality score of 4.9 from 6 ratings, and DaTarvia Parrish in History with a quality score of 5.0 from 2 ratings. This source is useful for answering questions about which professors appear to have strong student feedback, which departments have rated professors, and how students describe professor difficulty.

title: Niche - Livingstone College Overview
text: Niche describes Livingstone College as a private, Methodist, historically Black college located in Salisbury, North Carolina. The page gives Livingstone an overall grade of C and reports a rating of 3.38 out of 5 based on 615 reviews. It lists the acceptance rate as 59 percent and the total undergraduate enrollment as 924 students. Niche describes Livingstone as a small institution with a full-time undergraduate enrollment of 909 and part-time undergraduate enrollment of 15. Popular majors listed include Business, Sport and Fitness Management, and Biology. Niche also shows category grades such as Diversity B, Athletics B, Party Scene B+, Professors C+, Dorms B minus, Campus Food C, Student Life C+, and Safety C+. This source is useful for general questions about Livingstone's size, location, student experience, academics, cost, and campus quality.

title: Niche - Livingstone College Reviews
text: The Niche reviews page summarizes student opinions about Livingstone College. The page shows an overall review rating of about 3.38 out of 5 based on 615 reviews. Niche's student review summary says students often describe Livingstone as close-knit, supportive, and helpful for academic and personal growth. Reviews mention approachable professors and staff, smaller class sizes, school pride, diversity, and a welcoming environment. At the same time, students also mention areas that could improve, including campus organization, communication, facilities, dining options, academic and career resources, student activities, transportation, and housing. Some reviews say the college helped students grow and build relationships, while others say students need to stay proactive and advocate for themselves. This source is useful for questions about student satisfaction, strengths, weaknesses, and common student complaints.

title: Niche - Livingstone College Campus Life
text: Niche's campus life page gives Livingstone College a Party Scene grade of B+, Safety grade of C+, Dorms grade of B minus, and Campus Food grade of C. The page says on-campus housing is available and that freshmen are not required to live on campus, although 93 percent of freshmen live on campus. It lists the average housing cost as ,262 per year and the average meal plan cost as ,531 per year. Student polls on the page say 36 percent of students rate overall dorm quality as great and 45 percent say the dorm social atmosphere is great. For campus community, 25 percent say they love everything about the school and have a lot of campus pride, while 53 percent say they like mostly everything but wish some things were different. Food poll responses mention options such as Naples Cafe, Sonic, Italy Cafe, Thelma's, and the cafe.

title: Appily - Livingstone College Reviews
text: Appily lists Livingstone College as a private four-year college located in Salisbury, North Carolina. Its review page includes student comments about the college experience. One visible review describes Livingstone as a good HBCU and mentions basketball opportunities. Another student review says Livingstone has a student-friendly campus environment with staff and professors who are willing to help students reach their academic goals. Another review says attending Livingstone taught the student about college and life in general, not only academics. This source is useful because it gives another student-review perspective outside of Niche. It can help answer questions about whether students feel supported, how students describe the environment, and what kind of personal growth students connect with the school.

title: Reddit r/HBCU - Livingstone College Thread
source_type: Reddit advice thread / informal student guidance
text: A Reddit thread in r/HBCU about Livingstone College includes informal advice for a student asking about the school. The original post was deleted, but comments remain. One commenter tells the student they will be fine and will find their group. The advice says that HBCUs can be historically underfunded and short staffed, so students may experience slower processes. The commenter recommends keeping copies of financial aid letters and award documents, following up even if it means going to the financial aid office in person, extending grace while still holding people accountable, being aware that dorms may not always be the nicest, and staying open to making friends. Another comment notes that from a sports standpoint, Livingstone is a Division II school in the CIAA conference. This source is useful for practical survival advice that may not appear on official school pages.

title: Livingstone College Student Affairs
text: Livingstone College's Student Affairs page says the Office of Student Affairs is located in the W.J. Walls Center on the third floor at 800 W. Thomas Street in Salisbury, North Carolina. The office hours are Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. The page lists student-facing areas such as Residence Life, Health Services, Student Activities, Counseling Services, Career Readiness Institute, Success Center, and Spiritual Life. The mission text says Student Affairs supports students from orientation to graduation through programs, services, activities, resources, advocacy, problem resolution, and crisis intervention. It also says Student Affairs helps students apply classroom learning to real-life practice through student organizations, service learning, and leadership development. This source is useful for questions about where students can go for support, campus resources, leadership opportunities, and student problem solving.

title: Livingstone College Residence Life
text: Livingstone College's Residence Life page says the Residence Life office is located in the W.J. Walls Center on the second floor at 800 W. Thomas Street in Salisbury, North Carolina. The page lists an on-call phone number and identifies the Dean of Residence Life. The Residence Life section explains that campus housing is connected to student social development and that the college wants students to live, learn, and mature in an environment that supports self-respect, self-direction, social dignity, and academic progress. Students must be admitted to the college and pay required non-refundable prepayments before a housing application is accepted. The housing application must be submitted each year. Accepting a housing assignment means the student agrees to the terms and conditions of the assignment, and failure to follow residence life rules may lead to loss of housing privileges. Residence halls are managed by hall directors, supervisors, and resident assistants.

title: Livingstone College Campus Life
text: Livingstone College's official Campus Life page presents campus life as a mix of friendship, activities, housing, Greek life, learning communities, food, and local Salisbury experiences. The page says there are clubs and activities that can become both resume highlights and lifelong memories. It links students to clubs and activities, housing information, Greek life, and learning communities. The page also highlights food and says students should explore Livingstone's Culinary Arts program options. It includes a section about exploring Livingstone and the surrounding area, mentioning local attractions such as Piedmont Players Theatre and Piedmont Skydiving. This source is useful for questions about what students can do outside class, how the college describes involvement, and what official campus life resources are available.

title: LuxeLife Dining - Livingstone Meal Plans
text: The LuxeLife Dining meal plan page for Livingstone College describes resident and commuter meal plans. It says meal plans can be used at the all-you-care-to-eat City Market or City Market Takeout, with dining dollars usable in retail locations. The page says resident students can sign up for a meal plan through Auxiliary Services on the second floor of the Student Service Center. The resident option shown is a 19-meal plan, which gives students 19 meals per week in the dining hall from Monday through Sunday. The commuter meal plan section says commuter plans are meant to be convenient and budget friendly, are valid for one semester, and are non-transferable. The FAQ says students may upgrade their meal plan during the first two weeks of the semester, unused meals do not carry over week to week, refunds are not given for leftover meals, and meal plans cannot be used to treat a guest during the same meal period.

title: LuxeLife Dining - Livingstone Dining Menu
text: The Livingstone dining menu page did not load usable menu text during collection, so this document records dining-related information from accessible dining and campus-food sources. LuxeLife Dining says Livingstone meal plans can be used at City Market or City Market Takeout, and describes the dining hall as an all-you-care-to-eat location with comfort foods, pizza, grilled sandwiches, and vegetarian options. It also says dining dollars or bonus points may be used in retail locations. Niche's campus life page gives Livingstone campus food a C grade and reports that meal plans are available. Student poll responses on Niche mention food options or nearby options such as Naples Cafe, Sonic, Italy Cafe, Thelma's, the cafe, Cook Out, McDonald's, and other local food places. This source is useful for questions about what food options students mention, but it may not answer questions about the exact menu for a specific day.
